coverage: 100% of reachable node types; the browser evaluates all 1,007 nodes in the root closure.
validation: Four Blender/VM cases cover authored settings, wrapper switching, thickness, and wrapper dimensions. The portable dump preserves every float32 coordinate of the authored drawing curve and Grill Teef target, Resample Curve reproduces Blender's cached final-segment factor, and Raycast direction normalization plus all 6,213 target points are bit-exact. Point Geometry Proximity now also matches all 52,555 Blender distances bit-for-bit by reproducing Blender's FLT_EPSILON-inflated BVH leaves; this improves thickness 6 from 42,019 / 33,430 to 42,027 / 33,436 versus Blender's 42,031 / 33,434 without changing Bubble Putty. The authored result remains 61,799 / 53,892 versus Blender's 61,812 / 53,892 with a 0.0018-unit maximum bounds difference, wrapper-enabled is identical, and wrapper 60 by 45 remains 38,338 / 33,678 versus 38,301 / 33,678 with a 0.0002-unit bounds difference. The prior proximity-distance localization is therefore ruled out for the final authored 13-vertex Auto Smooth residual; its first divergence must be re-probed inside the downstream marching-squares/sharpen chain. Applying the browser Edge Angle rule to Blender's exact pre-split mesh still produces Blender's exact 61,812 vertices, so Split Edges and its angle threshold also remain ruled out.
